response.original_title: Why is Lost Highway the greatest film David Lynch ever made?
response.overview: Lost Highway is often considered the most “Lynchian” film by David Lynch because it fully embraces surreal storytelling, psychological horror, and fractured identity. The film doesn’t try to explain itself logically—it makes you feel the confusion, guilt, and paranoia of the main character.  It also has one of the boldest narrative ideas in cinema: a man seemingly transforming into another person as a psychological escape. Combined with the terrifying Mystery Man character (played by Robert Blake), the film creates a haunting, dream-like experience that stays in your mind long after it ends.  Many fans argue that this film captures Lynch’s themes—identity, guilt, and the darkness under reality—more purely than his other movies like Mulholland Drive or Blue Velvet.
response.title: Why is Lost Highway the greatest film David Lynch ever made?
